title: Flux Image Generation Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands and techniques for using Flux in image generation tasks.

commands.description: Generates an image with an impressionist style.
commands.scenario: When creating artwork for a digital gallery with a specific art style.

commands.description: Sets the API key for authenticating with the Flux service.
commands.scenario: When configuring the CLI tool for first-time use or after a key rotation.

commands.description: Specifies the resolution for the generated image.
commands.scenario: When high-resolution images are needed for print or large displays.

commands.command: flux generate --prompt 'A futuristic cityscape at night'
commands.description: Generates an image based on a text prompt.
commands.scenario: When creating concept art based on specific themes or ideas.

commands.description: Selects the model version to use for image generation.
commands.scenario: When testing new model capabilities or comparing outputs between versions.

commands.description: Sets the output format of the generated image.
commands.scenario: When specific image formats are required for compatibility or size constraints.

commands.description: Streams the image generation process in real-time.
commands.scenario: When needing to monitor the progress of image generation for long-running tasks.

commands.description: Sets a seed for random number generation to ensure reproducibility.
commands.scenario: When needing consistent outputs across multiple runs for testing or comparison.

commands.description: Applies style transfer from a source image to the generated image.
commands.scenario: When blending styles from existing images into new creations.

commands.description: Generates a batch of images in one command.
commands.scenario: When needing multiple images quickly for a project or experiment.

commands.description: Uses a context file to influence the image generation process.
commands.scenario: When aligning generated images with a specific theme or project guidelines.

commands.description: Sets a cost limit for the image generation process.
commands.scenario: When operating under budget constraints for image generation tasks.

commands.description: Fine-tune the balance between prompt adherence and creative freedom by adjusting guidance and CFG scales independently.
Perform image-to-image refinements by specifying elements to exclude and controlling the amount of structural change.
Optimize generation quality and speed by selecting specific sampling algorithms and defining iteration depth.
Analyze API response times and token usage to optimize cost and identify bottlenecks in the generation pipeline.
Use structural conditioning to force the model to follow specific geometric outlines or edge maps from a reference image.
